revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宣旨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11452942]] Wikidata name: senji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|[[:en:Emperor of Japan|Imperial edicts and official documents]] or oral orders by aristocrats|The title of a senior female servant or {{ill|nyōbō|ja|女房|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nyōbō|qq=|1=nyōbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856232}}|{{ill|Senji (official position)|ja|宣旨 (役職)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji (official position)|qq=|1=senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97175611}}}} '''Senji''' (宣旨) refers to the format of documents used to transmit the orders of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] or the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] in post-{{ill|Ritsuryō period|ja|律令期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q742460}} Japan. It is one of the document forms issued by the imperial court, a variant of {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}}.
== Overview == The [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]'s orders or intentions ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|qq=|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) were transcribed into documents such as {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|qq=|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} and {{ill|Daijōkanchō|ja|太政官牒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanchō|qq=|1=Daijō-kan chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444011}} by the {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, with {{ill|Shi (Ritsuryō)|ja|史 (律令制)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi (Ritsuryō)|qq=}} staff receiving the emperor's commands orally. To avoid forgetting the orders, scribes created personal notes, which gradually evolved into formalized documents called Senji. While only the signatures of the scribe and other officials appeared on the documents, they were recognized as reflecting the emperor’s will. This conferred authority on unsigned documents, occasionally leading to the issuance of {{ill|Fake imperial edicts|ja|偽勅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fake imperial edicts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period.
Initially, imperial commands were issued as {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔}} or {{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|qq=|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}, which were reserved for significant matters and strictly regulated in form. To allow for greater flexibility, the Senji format was introduced.
=== Issuance Procedure === The issuance procedure involved conveying the emperor's command through the {{ill|Naishi no Tsukasa|ja|内侍司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi no Tsukasa|qq=|1=Naishi no Tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, followed by the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|qq=|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}, and then to the appointed {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|上卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=|1=Jōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358535}}. The Jōkyō would transmit it to relevant offices, including the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}}, {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, and {{ill|Naiki-kyoku|ja|内記局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki-kyoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 内記|1=naiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394590}}, which would draft the Senji. This process began during the [[:en:Kōnin (era)|Kōnin era]] (810–824). Commands passed from the Naishi to the Kurododokoro were called {{ill|Naishi-sen|ja|内侍宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-sen|qq=|1=naishi-sen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393858}} and later evolved into {{ill|Nyōbō Hōsho|ja|女房奉書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyōbō Hōsho|qq=|1=nyōbō hōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446474}} in the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. Oral orders from the Kurododokoro to the Jōkyō, known as {{ill|Kusenshō|ja|口宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō|qq=|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}}, were later documented and su11lemented with {{ill|Kusenshō-an|ja|口宣案|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō-an|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 口宣|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}} as drafts.
By the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Senji issued by the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} in their name were recognized. Additionally, documents issued by the Benkankyoku on the order of high-Ranking officials ({{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}}) instead of the {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|qq=|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} were called {{ill|Kan-senji|ja|官宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-senji|qq=|1=kan-senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452102}}.
=== Examples of Senji === Examples of Senji include:
* '''Concurrent Official Appointment (Yamakai-ki)''': 内大臣藤原朝臣 正二位行大納言源朝臣雅通宣 奉 勅、件人宜如旧兼任右近大将者 仁安二年二月十一日 大外記 (Translation): Minister of the Interior Fujiwara no Ason declares: By imperial decree, the aforementioned person is to continue serving concurrently as Ukon no Dainagon. February 11, 1167, by the Great External Scribe.
* '''Acting Regent Senji (Koryū-ki)''': 正二位行権大納言兼太皇太后宮大夫藤原朝臣公任宣 奉 勅、除目等雑事、宜令左大臣准摂政儀行之者 長和四年十月廿七日 大外記小野朝臣文義奉 (Translation): Senior Second Rank Acting Chancellor and Minister to the Dowager Empress' Palace Fujiwara no Ason Kintō declares: By imperial decree, the Left Minister shall perform the duties of a regent for appointments and miscellaneous matters. October 27, 1015, by the Great External Scribe Ono no Ason Fumiyoshi.
=== Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|qq=|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|qq=|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|qq=|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|qq=|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|qq=|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|qq=|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|qq=|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|qq=|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|qq=|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|qq=|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|qq=|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}
== Further Reading == * Shohachi Hayakawa, *Senji Shiron*, Iwanami Shoten, April 1990, ISBN 4-00-001193-6.

Initially, imperial commands were issued as {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔}} or {{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|qq=|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}, which were reserved for significant matters and strictly regulated in form. === Issuance Procedure === The issuance procedure involved conveying the emperor's command through the {{ill|Naishi no Tsukasa|ja|内侍司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi no Tsukasa|qq=|1=Naishi no Tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, followed by the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|qq=|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}, and then to the appointed {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|上卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=|1=Jōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358535}}. The Jōkyō would transmit it to relevant offices, including the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}}, {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, and {{ill|Naiki-kyoku|ja|内記局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki-kyoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 内記|1=naiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394590}}, which would draft the Senji. By the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Senji issued by the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} in their name were recognized. Additionally, documents issued by the Benkankyoku on the order of high-Ranking officials ({{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}}) instead of the {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|qq=|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} were called {{ill|Kan-senji|ja|官宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-senji|qq=|1=kan-senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452102}}.

* '''Concurrent Official Appointment (Yamakai-ki)''': 内大臣藤原朝臣 正二位行大納言源朝臣雅通宣 奉 勅、件人宜如旧兼任右近大将者 仁安二年二月十一日 大外記 (Translation): Minister of the Interior Fujiwara no Ason declares: By imperial decree, the aforementioned person is to continue serving concurrently as Ukon no Dainagon. February 11, 1167, by the Great External Scribe.
* '''Acting Regent Senji (Koryū-ki)''': 正二位行権大納言兼太皇太后宮大夫藤原朝臣公任宣 奉 勅、除目等雑事、宜令左大臣准摂政儀行之者 長和四年十月廿七日 大外記小野朝臣文義奉 (Translation): Senior Second Rank Acting Chancellor and Minister to the Dowager Empress' Palace Fujiwara no Ason Kintō declares: By imperial decree, the Left Minister shall perform the duties of a regent for appointments and miscellaneous matters. October 27, 1015, by the Great External Scribe Ono no Ason Fumiyoshi.
=== Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|qq=|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|qq=|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|qq=|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|qq=|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|qq=|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|qq=|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|qq=|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|qq=|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|qq=|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|qq=|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|qq=|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}

<!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--> [[Category:Diplomatics]] [[Category:Official order documents]] [[Category:Imperial Proclamations]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[de:Senji]] {{wikidata link|Q11452942}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宣旨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452942]] Wikidata name: senji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|[[:en:Emperor of Japan|Imperial edicts and official documents]] or oral orders by aristocrats|The title of a senior female servant or {{ill|nyōbō|ja|女房|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nyōbō|1=nyōbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856232}}|{{ill|Senji (official position)|ja|宣旨 (役職)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji (official position)|1=senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97175611}}}} '''Senji''' (宣旨) refers to the format of documents used to transmit the orders of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] or the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] in post-{{ill|Ritsuryō period|ja|律令期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q742460}} Japan. It is one of the document forms issued by the imperial court, a variant of {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}}.
== Overview == The [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]'s orders or intentions ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) were transcribed into documents such as {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} and {{ill|Daijōkanchō|ja|太政官牒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanchō|1=Daijō-kan chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444011}} by the {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, with {{ill|Shi (Ritsuryō)|ja|史 (律令制)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi (Ritsuryō)|qq=}} staff receiving the emperor's commands orally. To avoid forgetting the orders, scribes created personal notes, which gradually evolved into formalized documents called Senji. While only the signatures of the scribe and other officials appeared on the documents, they were recognized as reflecting the emperor’s will. This conferred authority on unsigned documents, occasionally leading to the issuance of {{ill|Fake imperial edicts|ja|偽勅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fake imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period.
Initially, imperial commands were issued as {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔}} or {{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}, which were reserved for significant matters and strictly regulated in form. To allow for greater flexibility, the Senji format was introduced.
=== Issuance Procedure === The issuance procedure involved conveying the emperor's command through the {{ill|Naishi no Tsukasa|ja|内侍司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi no Tsukasa|1=Naishi no Tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, followed by the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}, and then to the appointed {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|上卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358535}}. The Jōkyō would transmit it to relevant offices, including the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}}, {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, and {{ill|Naiki-kyoku|ja|内記局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 内記|1=naiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394590}}, which would draft the Senji. This process began during the [[:en:Kōnin (era)|Kōnin era]] (810–824). Commands passed from the Naishi to the Kurododokoro were called {{ill|Naishi-sen|ja|内侍宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-sen|1=naishi-sen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393858}} and later evolved into {{ill|Nyōbō Hōsho|ja|女房奉書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyōbō Hōsho|1=nyōbō hōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446474}} in the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. Oral orders from the Kurododokoro to the Jōkyō, known as {{ill|Kusenshō|ja|口宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}}, were later documented and su11lemented with {{ill|Kusenshō-an|ja|口宣案|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō-an|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 口宣|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}} as drafts.
By the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Senji issued by the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} in their name were recognized. Additionally, documents issued by the Benkankyoku on the order of high-Ranking officials ({{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}}) instead of the {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} were called {{ill|Kan-senji|ja|官宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-senji|1=kan-senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452102}}.
=== Examples of Senji === Examples of Senji include:
* '''Concurrent Official Appointment (Yamakai-ki)''': 内大臣藤原朝臣 正二位行大納言源朝臣雅通宣 奉 勅、件人宜如旧兼任右近大将者 仁安二年二月十一日 大外記 (Translation): Minister of the Interior Fujiwara no Ason declares: By imperial decree, the aforementioned person is to continue serving concurrently as Ukon no Dainagon. February 11, 1167, by the Great External Scribe.
* '''Acting Regent Senji (Koryū-ki)''': 正二位行権大納言兼太皇太后宮大夫藤原朝臣公任宣 奉 勅、除目等雑事、宜令左大臣准摂政儀行之者 長和四年十月廿七日 大外記小野朝臣文義奉 (Translation): Senior Second Rank Acting Chancellor and Minister to the Dowager Empress' Palace Fujiwara no Ason Kintō declares: By imperial decree, the Left Minister shall perform the duties of a regent for appointments and miscellaneous matters. October 27, 1015, by the Great External Scribe Ono no Ason Fumiyoshi.
=== Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}

* '''Concurrent Official Appointment (Yamakai-ki)''': 内大臣藤原朝臣 正二位行大納言源朝臣雅通宣 奉 勅、件人宜如旧兼任右近大将者 仁安二年二月十一日 大外記 (Translation): Minister of the Interior Fujiwara no Ason declares: By imperial decree, the aforementioned person is to continue serving concurrently as Ukon no Dainagon. === Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}
== Further Reading == * Shohachi Hayakawa, *Senji Shiron*, Iwanami Shoten, April 1990, ISBN 4-00-001193-6.

[[ja:宣旨]] [[en:Senji]]
<!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--> [[Category:Diplomatics]] [[Category:Official order documents]] [[Category:Imperial Proclamations]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[de:Senji]] {{wikidata link|Q11452942}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宣旨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452942]] Wikidata name: senji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|[[:en:Emperor of Japan|Imperial edicts and official documents]] or oral orders by aristocrats|The title of a senior female servant or {{ill|nyōbō|ja|女房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nyōbō|1=nyōbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856232}}|{{ill|Senji (official position)|ja|宣旨 (役職)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji (official position)|1=senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97175611}}}} '''Senji''' (宣旨) refers to the format of documents used to transmit the orders of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] or the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] in post-{{ill|Ritsuryō period|ja|律令期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q742460}} Japan. It is one of the document forms issued by the imperial court, a variant of {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}}.
== Overview == The [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]'s orders or intentions ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) were transcribed into documents such as {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} and {{ill|Daijōkanchō|ja|太政官牒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanchō|1=Daijō-kan chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444011}} by the {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, with {{ill|Shi (Ritsuryō)|ja|史 (律令制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi (Ritsuryō)|qq=}} staff receiving the emperor's commands orally. To avoid forgetting the orders, scribes created personal notes, which gradually evolved into formalized documents called Senji. While only the signatures of the scribe and other officials appeared on the documents, they were recognized as reflecting the emperor’s will. This conferred authority on unsigned documents, occasionally leading to the issuance of {{ill|Fake imperial edicts|ja|偽勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fake imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period.
Initially, imperial commands were issued as {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔}} or {{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}, which were reserved for significant matters and strictly regulated in form. To allow for greater flexibility, the Senji format was introduced.
=== Issuance Procedure === The issuance procedure involved conveying the emperor's command through the {{ill|Naishi no Tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi no Tsukasa|1=Naishi no Tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, followed by the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}, and then to the appointed {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|上卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358535}}. The Jōkyō would transmit it to relevant offices, including the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}}, {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, and {{ill|Naiki-kyoku|ja|内記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 内記|1=naiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394590}}, which would draft the Senji. This process began during the [[:en:Kōnin (era)|Kōnin era]] (810–824). Commands passed from the Naishi to the Kurododokoro were called {{ill|Naishi-sen|ja|内侍宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-sen|1=naishi-sen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393858}} and later evolved into {{ill|Nyōbō Hōsho|ja|女房奉書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyōbō Hōsho|1=nyōbō hōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446474}} in the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. Oral orders from the Kurododokoro to the Jōkyō, known as {{ill|Kusenshō|ja|口宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}}, were later documented and su11lemented with {{ill|Kusenshō-an|ja|口宣案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō-an|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 口宣|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}} as drafts.
By the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Senji issued by the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} in their name were recognized. Additionally, documents issued by the Benkankyoku on the order of high-Ranking officials ({{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}}) instead of the {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} were called {{ill|Kan-senji|ja|官宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-senji|1=kan-senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452102}}.
=== Examples of Senji === Examples of Senji include:
* '''Concurrent Official Appointment (Yamakai-ki)''': 内大臣藤原朝臣 正二位行大納言源朝臣雅通宣 奉 勅、件人宜如旧兼任右近大将者 仁安二年二月十一日 大外記 (Translation): Minister of the Interior Fujiwara no Ason declares: By imperial decree, the aforementioned person is to continue serving concurrently as Ukon no Dainagon. February 11, 1167, by the Great External Scribe.
* '''Acting Regent Senji (Koryū-ki)''': 正二位行権大納言兼太皇太后宮大夫藤原朝臣公任宣 奉 勅、除目等雑事、宜令左大臣准摂政儀行之者 長和四年十月廿七日 大外記小野朝臣文義奉 (Translation): Senior Second Rank Acting Chancellor and Minister to the Dowager Empress' Palace Fujiwara no Ason Kintō declares: By imperial decree, the Left Minister shall perform the duties of a regent for appointments and miscellaneous matters. October 27, 1015, by the Great External Scribe Ono no Ason Fumiyoshi.
=== Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}
== Further Reading == * Shohachi Hayakawa, *Senji Shiron*, Iwanami Shoten, April 1990, ISBN 4-00-001193-6.

=== Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}
== Further Reading == * Shohachi Hayakawa, *Senji Shiron*, Iwanami Shoten, April 1990, ISBN 4-00-001193-6.

<!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--> [[Category:Diplomatics]] [[Category:Official order documents]] [[Category:Imperial Proclamations]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[de:Senji]] {{wikidata link|Q11452942}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宣旨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452942]] Wikidata name: senji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|[[:en:Emperor of Japan|Imperial edicts and official documents]] or oral orders by aristocrats|The title of a senior female servant or {{ill|nyōbō|ja|女房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nyōbō|1=nyōbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856232}}|{{ill|Senji (official position)|ja|宣旨 (役職)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji (official position)|1=senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97175611}}}} '''Senji''' (宣旨) refers to the format of documents used to transmit the orders of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] or the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] in post-{{ill|Ritsuryō period|ja|律令期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q742460}} Japan. It is one of the document forms issued by the imperial court, a variant of {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}}.
== Overview == The [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]'s orders or intentions ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) were transcribed into documents such as {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} and {{ill|Daijōkanchō|ja|太政官牒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanchō|1=Daijō-kan chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444011}} by the {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, with {{ill|Shi (Ritsuryō)|ja|史 (律令制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi (Ritsuryō)|qq=}} staff receiving the emperor's commands orally. To avoid forgetting the orders, scribes created personal notes, which gradually evolved into formalized documents called Senji. While only the signatures of the scribe and other officials appeared on the documents, they were recognized as reflecting the emperor’s will. This conferred authority on unsigned documents, occasionally leading to the issuance of {{ill|Fake imperial edicts|ja|偽勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fake imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period.
Initially, imperial commands were issued as {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔}} or {{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}, which were reserved for significant matters and strictly regulated in form. To allow for greater flexibility, the Senji format was introduced.
=== Issuance Procedure === The issuance procedure involved conveying the emperor's command through the {{ill|Naishi no Tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi no Tsukasa|1=Naishi no Tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, followed by the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}, and then to the appointed {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|上卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358535}}. The Jōkyō would transmit it to relevant offices, including the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}}, {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, and {{ill|Naiki-kyoku|ja|内記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 内記|1=naiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394590}}, which would draft the Senji. This process began during the [[:en:Kōnin (era)|Kōnin era]] (810–824). Commands passed from the Naishi to the Kurododokoro were called {{ill|Naishi-sen|ja|内侍宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-sen|1=naishi-sen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393858}} and later evolved into {{ill|Nyōbō Hōsho|ja|女房奉書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyōbō Hōsho|1=nyōbō hōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446474}} in the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. Oral orders from the Kurododokoro to the Jōkyō, known as {{ill|Kusenshō|ja|口宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}}, were later documented and su11lemented with {{ill|Kusenshō-an|ja|口宣案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō-an|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 口宣|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}} as drafts.
By the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Senji issued by the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} in their name were recognized. Additionally, documents issued by the Benkankyoku on the order of high-Ranking officials ({{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}}) instead of the {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} were called {{ill|Kan-senji|ja|官宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-senji|1=kan-senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452102}}.
=== Examples of Senji === Examples of Senji include:
* '''Concurrent Official Appointment (Yamakai-ki)''': 内大臣藤原朝臣 正二位行大納言源朝臣雅通宣 奉 勅、件人宜如旧兼任右近大将者 仁安二年二月十一日 大外記 (Translation): Minister of the Interior Fujiwara no Ason declares: By imperial decree, the aforementioned person is to continue serving concurrently as Ukon no Dainagon. February 11, 1167, by the Great External Scribe.
* '''Acting Regent Senji (Koryū-ki)''': 正二位行権大納言兼太皇太后宮大夫藤原朝臣公任宣 奉 勅、除目等雑事、宜令左大臣准摂政儀行之者 長和四年十月廿七日 大外記小野朝臣文義奉 (Translation): Senior Second Rank Acting Chancellor and Minister to the Dowager Empress' Palace Fujiwara no Ason Kintō declares: By imperial decree, the Left Minister shall perform the duties of a regent for appointments and miscellaneous matters. October 27, 1015, by the Great External Scribe Ono no Ason Fumiyoshi.
=== Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}
== Further Reading == * Shohachi Hayakawa, *Senji Shiron*, Iwanami Shoten, April 1990, ISBN 4-00-001193-6.

Additionally, documents issued by the Benkankyoku on the order of high-Ranking officials ({{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}}) instead of the {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} were called {{ill|Kan-senji|ja|官宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-senji|1=kan-senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452102}}.

* '''Concurrent Official Appointment (Yamakai-ki)''': 内大臣藤原朝臣 正二位行大納言源朝臣雅通宣 奉 勅、件人宜如旧兼任右近大将者 仁安二年二月十一日 大外記 (Translation): Minister of the Interior Fujiwara no Ason declares: By imperial decree, the aforementioned person is to continue serving concurrently as Ukon no Dainagon. February 11, 1167, by the Great External Scribe.
* '''Acting Regent Senji (Koryū-ki)''': 正二位行権大納言兼太皇太后宮大夫藤原朝臣公任宣 奉 勅、除目等雑事、宜令左大臣准摂政儀行之者 長和四年十月廿七日 大外記小野朝臣文義奉 (Translation): Senior Second Rank Acting Chancellor and Minister to the Dowager Empress' Palace Fujiwara no Ason Kintō declares: By imperial decree, the Left Minister shall perform the duties of a regent for appointments and miscellaneous matters. October 27, 1015, by the Great External Scribe Ono no Ason Fumiyoshi.
=== Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}

<!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--> [[Category:Diplomatics]] [[Category:Official order documents]] [[Category:Imperial Proclamations]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[de:Senji]] {{wikidata link|Q11452942}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宣旨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452942]] Wikidata name: senji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|[[:en:Emperor of Japan|Imperial edicts and official documents]] or oral orders by aristocrats|The title of a senior female servant or {{ill|nyōbō|ja|女房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nyōbō|1=nyōbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856232}}|{{ill|Senji (official position)|ja|宣旨 (役職)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji (official position)|1=senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97175611}}}} '''Senji''' (宣旨) refers to the format of documents used to transmit the orders of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] or the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] in post-{{ill|Ritsuryō period|ja|律令期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q742460}} Japan. It is one of the document forms issued by the imperial court, a variant of {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}}.
== Overview == The [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]'s orders or intentions ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) were transcribed into documents such as {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} and {{ill|Daijōkanchō|ja|太政官牒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanchō|1=Daijō-kan chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444011}} by the {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, with {{ill|Shi (Ritsuryō)|ja|史 (律令制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi (Ritsuryō)|qq=}} staff receiving the emperor's commands orally. To avoid forgetting the orders, scribes created personal notes, which gradually evolved into formalized documents called Senji. While only the signatures of the scribe and other officials appeared on the documents, they were recognized as reflecting the emperor’s will. This conferred authority on unsigned documents, occasionally leading to the issuance of {{ill|Fake imperial edicts|ja|偽勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fake imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period.
Initially, imperial commands were issued as {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔}} or {{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}, which were reserved for significant matters and strictly regulated in form. To allow for greater flexibility, the Senji format was introduced.
=== Issuance Procedure === The issuance procedure involved conveying the emperor's command through the {{ill|Naishi no Tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi no Tsukasa|1=Naishi no Tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, followed by the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}, and then to the appointed {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|上卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358535}}. The Jōkyō would transmit it to relevant offices, including the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}}, {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, and {{ill|Naiki-kyoku|ja|内記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 内記|1=naiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394590}}, which would draft the Senji. This process began during the [[:en:Kōnin (era)|Kōnin era]] (810–824). Commands passed from the Naishi to the Kurododokoro were called {{ill|Naishi-sen|ja|内侍宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-sen|1=naishi-sen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393858}} and later evolved into {{ill|Nyōbō Hōsho|ja|女房奉書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyōbō Hōsho|1=nyōbō hōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446474}} in the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. Oral orders from the Kurododokoro to the Jōkyō, known as {{ill|Kusenshō|ja|口宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}}, were later documented and su11lemented with {{ill|Kusenshō-an|ja|口宣案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō-an|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 口宣|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}} as drafts.
By the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Senji issued by the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} in their name were recognized. Additionally, documents issued by the Benkankyoku on the order of high-Ranking officials ({{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}}) instead of the {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} were called {{ill|Kan-senji|ja|官宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-senji|1=kan-senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452102}}.
=== Examples of Senji === Examples of Senji include:
* '''Concurrent Official Appointment (Yamakai-ki)''': 内大臣藤原朝臣 正二位行大納言源朝臣雅通宣 奉 勅、件人宜如旧兼任右近大将者 仁安二年二月十一日 大外記 (Translation): Minister of the Interior Fujiwara no Ason declares: By imperial decree, the aforementioned person is to continue serving concurrently as Ukon no Dainagon. February 11, 1167, by the Great External Scribe.
* '''Acting Regent Senji (Koryū-ki)''': 正二位行権大納言兼太皇太后宮大夫藤原朝臣公任宣 奉 勅、除目等雑事、宜令左大臣准摂政儀行之者 長和四年十月廿七日 大外記小野朝臣文義奉 (Translation): Senior Second Rank Acting Chancellor and Minister to the Dowager Empress' Palace Fujiwara no Ason Kintō declares: By imperial decree, the Left Minister shall perform the duties of a regent for appointments and miscellaneous matters. October 27, 1015, by the Great External Scribe Ono no Ason Fumiyoshi.

Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}

=== Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}
== Further Reading == * Shohachi Hayakawa, *Senji Shiron*, Iwanami Shoten, April 1990, ISBN 4-00-001193-6.

<!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--><!--Senji--> [[Category:Diplomatics]] [[Category:Official order documents]] [[Category:Imperial Proclamations]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[de:Senji]] {{wikidata link|Q11452942}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宣旨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452942]] Wikidata name: senji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|[[:en:Emperor of Japan|Imperial edicts and official documents]] or oral orders by aristocrats|The title of a senior female servant or {{ill|nyōbō|ja|女房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nyōbō|1=nyōbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856232}}|{{ill|Senji (official position)|ja|宣旨 (役職)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji (official position)|1=senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97175611}}}} '''Senji''' (宣旨) refers to the format of documents used to transmit the orders of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] or the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]] in post-{{ill|Ritsuryō period|ja|律令期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q742460}} Japan. It is one of the document forms issued by the imperial court, a variant of {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}}.
== Overview == The [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]'s orders or intentions ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) were transcribed into documents such as {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} and {{ill|Daijōkanchō|ja|太政官牒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanchō|1=Daijō-kan chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444011}} by the {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, with {{ill|Shi (Ritsuryō)|ja|史 (律令制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi (Ritsuryō)|qq=}} staff receiving the emperor's commands orally. To avoid forgetting the orders, scribes created personal notes, which gradually evolved into formalized documents called Senji. While only the signatures of the scribe and other officials appeared on the documents, they were recognized as reflecting the emperor’s will. This conferred authority on unsigned documents, occasionally leading to the issuance of {{ill|Fake imperial edicts|ja|偽勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fake imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period.
Initially, imperial commands were issued as {{ill|Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial decrees|lt=Imperial edicts|ja|詔書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 詔}} or {{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}, which were reserved for significant matters and strictly regulated in form. To allow for greater flexibility, the Senji format was introduced.
=== Issuance Procedure === The issuance procedure involved conveying the emperor's command through the {{ill|Naishi no Tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi no Tsukasa|1=Naishi no Tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, followed by the {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}, and then to the appointed {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|上卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358535}}. The Jōkyō would transmit it to relevant offices, including the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}}, {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, and {{ill|Naiki-kyoku|ja|内記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 内記|1=naiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394590}}, which would draft the Senji. This process began during the [[:en:Kōnin (era)|Kōnin era]] (810–824). Commands passed from the Naishi to the Kurododokoro were called {{ill|Naishi-sen|ja|内侍宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-sen|1=naishi-sen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393858}} and later evolved into {{ill|Nyōbō Hōsho|ja|女房奉書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyōbō Hōsho|1=nyōbō hōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446474}} in the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. Oral orders from the Kurododokoro to the Jōkyō, known as {{ill|Kusenshō|ja|口宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}}, were later documented and su11lemented with {{ill|Kusenshō-an|ja|口宣案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō-an|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 口宣|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}} as drafts.
By the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Senji issued by the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}} in their name were recognized. Additionally, documents issued by the Benkankyoku on the order of high-Ranking officials ({{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}}) instead of the {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} were called {{ill|Kan-senji|ja|官宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-senji|1=kan-senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452102}}.
=== Examples of Senji === Examples of Senji include:
* '''Concurrent Official Appointment (Yamakai-ki)''': 内大臣藤原朝臣 正二位行大納言源朝臣雅通宣 奉 勅、件人宜如旧兼任右近大将者 仁安二年二月十一日 大外記 (Translation): Minister of the Interior Fujiwara no Ason declares: By imperial decree, the aforementioned person is to continue serving concurrently as Ukon no Dainagon. February 11, 1167, by the Great External Scribe.
* '''Acting Regent Senji (Koryū-ki)''': 正二位行権大納言兼太皇太后宮大夫藤原朝臣公任宣 奉 勅、除目等雑事、宜令左大臣准摂政儀行之者 長和四年十月廿七日 大外記小野朝臣文義奉 (Translation): Senior Second Rank Acting Chancellor and Minister to the Dowager Empress' Palace Fujiwara no Ason Kintō declares: By imperial decree, the Left Minister shall perform the duties of a regent for appointments and miscellaneous matters. October 27, 1015, by the Great External Scribe Ono no Ason Fumiyoshi.
=== Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}
== Further Reading == * Shohachi Hayakawa, *Senji Shiron*, Iwanami Shoten, April 1990, ISBN 4-00-001193-6.

== Overview == The [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]'s orders or intentions ({{ill|Chokushi|ja|勅旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi|1=chokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399926}}) were transcribed into documents such as {{ill|Daijōkanpu|ja|太政官符|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanpu|1=Daijō-kan-pu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444013}} and {{ill|Daijōkanchō|ja|太政官牒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkanchō|1=Daijō-kan chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444011}} by the {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, with {{ill|Shi (Ritsuryō)|ja|史 (律令制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi (Ritsuryō)|qq=}} staff receiving the emperor's commands orally. To avoid forgetting the orders, scribes created personal notes, which gradually evolved into formalized documents called Senji. While only the signatures of the scribe and other officials appeared on the documents, they were recognized as reflecting the emperor’s will. This conferred authority on unsigned documents, occasionally leading to the issuance of {{ill|Fake imperial edicts|ja|偽勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fake imperial edicts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period.

The Jōkyō would transmit it to relevant offices, including the {{ill|Geki-kyoku|ja|外記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外記|1=geki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430536}}, {{ill|Benkankyoku|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benkankyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}}, and {{ill|Naiki-kyoku|ja|内記局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naiki-kyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 内記|1=naiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394590}}, which would draft the Senji. This process began during the [[:en:Kōnin (era)|Kōnin era]] (810–824). Commands passed from the Naishi to the Kurododokoro were called {{ill|Naishi-sen|ja|内侍宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-sen|1=naishi-sen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393858}} and later evolved into {{ill|Nyōbō Hōsho|ja|女房奉書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyōbō Hōsho|1=nyōbō hōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446474}} in the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. Oral orders from the Kurododokoro to the Jōkyō, known as {{ill|Kusenshō|ja|口宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}}, were later documented and su11lemented with {{ill|Kusenshō-an|ja|口宣案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusenshō-an|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 口宣|1=kuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410777}} as drafts.

* '''Concurrent Official Appointment (Yamakai-ki)''': 内大臣藤原朝臣 正二位行大納言源朝臣雅通宣 奉 勅、件人宜如旧兼任右近大将者 仁安二年二月十一日 大外記 (Translation): Minister of the Interior Fujiwara no Ason declares: By imperial decree, the aforementioned person is to continue serving concurrently as Ukon no Dainagon. February 11, 1167, by the Great External Scribe.
* '''Acting Regent Senji (Koryū-ki)''': 正二位行権大納言兼太皇太后宮大夫藤原朝臣公任宣 奉 勅、除目等雑事、宜令左大臣准摂政儀行之者 長和四年十月廿七日 大外記小野朝臣文義奉 (Translation): Senior Second Rank Acting Chancellor and Minister to the Dowager Empress' Palace Fujiwara no Ason Kintō declares: By imperial decree, the Left Minister shall perform the duties of a regent for appointments and miscellaneous matters. October 27, 1015, by the Great External Scribe Ono no Ason Fumiyoshi.
=== Types of Senji === {{section-stub}}
== Insei and Rinji == {{main|{{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}}|{{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}}} During the [[:en:Cloistered rule|Insei]] period and the subsequent [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the issuance of {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} exceeded Senji. Additionally, {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}}, a simplified form of Senji, became more common. == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial decrees|ja|詔勅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial decrees|qq=}} * {{ill|Insen|ja|院宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insen|1=inzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658118}} * {{ill|Rinji|ja|綸旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinji|1=rinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607250}} * {{ill|Reijishi|ja|令旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reijishi|1=ryōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378318}} * {{ill|Gokyōsho|ja|御教書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokyōsho|1=migyōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489107}} * {{ill|Gonaisho|ja|御内書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonaisho|1=gonaisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488800}} * {{ill|Uda Insenji|ja|宇多院宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda Insenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|ja|寿永二年十月宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|1=Juei 2nd Year Tenth Month Senji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458333}} * {{ill|Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja|寿永三年宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juei 3rd Year Senji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kobunsho|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobunsho|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}}
